channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Buy SPCX. $135 is the absolute floor. It will be at $260 within 12 months. You will hate yourself if you don't get in no… — 159 replies — 636.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Buy SPCX. $135 is the absolute floor. It will be at $260 within 12 months. You will hate yourself if you don't get in no…

Open thread • Replies: 159 • Updated: 2026-07-23 15:06 UTC

OP

Buy SPCX. $135 is the absolute floor. It will be at $260 within 12 months. You will hate yourself if you don't get in now.

Early replies (first 3)

62483359
>>62483350
4%

62483366
>>62483359
?

62483376
>>62483366
!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62510630
>>62510541
>orbital data-centers

62510633
>>62510620
>Look honey, I insulted someone without getting bad karma, can you tell it to your boyfriend ?

62510646
>>62483350
>$260
no
>$150
likely. if you bought the ipo thats on you bro

62510653
>>62510633

62510659
>>62510653

62510692
>>62510541
do the math for me.

like actually:
How much does each launch cost?
take the total cost of an orbital data center and divide it by the total PB of the center (would use EB, but the math would be too unweildy)
be sure to include the costs of the solar power panels and anything else.
Remember that you will need abotu 2sq meters of panel per PB, so 10,000 sq meters of solar panels. the cost of the panels is relatively reasonable. the cost of getting them into space undamaged and then assembling them does not seem as reasonable
Now figure out how much income that data center makes per PB (on earth, the rental rate per PB is about $2500/month, but that includes $1000+ in power usage) and then you discount for profit for the renter.

so lets say it is a 5 EB orbital center, so a little over 6000 PB makes maybe $4.2 million/month

each launch costs abotu $75 million, so it would take 18 months of profiit to pay for one launch. I estimate it would take 18 months to pay for each launch.

Assume they get 50% more efficient at scale, that is still 9 months of operations per launch to equal one terrestrial data center.

Now factor in india launching at 10% the cost. How will space x make any money?


Now do the cost of repairs and upgrades. A benefit of a terrestrial datacenter is that you just send bob and achmed over in a truck to upgrade. Now do that in space.

62510713
>>62510620
cap ex is a type of ex.

ex burns cash

you don't burn ex

so you don't be "burning up capex"

you are burning up cash on capex

Bro - this is a /biz/ thread People should understand how to use terms like capex more precisely than "they insist in burning up CapEx on a dumb AI" when they should be saying :

>No one touches spacex because they burn up too much cash on capex for stupid AI.

62511526
>>62509948
Closed at $115. Fair value probably around at most $70

62512073
>>62510606

>the military doesn’t have access to the internet or probably an entire different network for only military interfaces

The cope is strong baggie

62512276
>>62512073
The military has their own satellites but they are much fewer in number and have less capability as a result. SpaceX is the first to build a megaconstellation, which was only feasible because of their advancements in reusable boosters.

The military actually wanted to build a megaconstellation for Brilliant Pebbles in the 1980s, but it just wasn't feasible with technology of the time.

62512552
>>62511526
more like 30 lmao

62512843
>>62512073
>the military doesn’t have access to the internet or probably an entire different network for only military interfaces
I know you're just being a pedantic faggot, but one of the biggest contracts starlink has is the US military. If you knew anything you'd be aware of the starlink controversy that happened not long ago regarding supplying/cutting off internet to conflict areas of the Ukraine.
Starlink is the one good product SpaceX has done that actually makes money.

62512847
Can we break new lows today boys?

62512852
>>62512552
If you want to see what SPCX chart will look kike for the next years/decades, look at FCEL (FuelCell. a chronically unprofitable green energy pump & dump. same profitability & pipedream profile. It's a perpetual destruction of value

62512871
You know it's a great stock when there's a baggie cope general on /biz/. This might as well be the start of a recurring thread. AI data centers...IN SPACE

62512950
floor is 112.67

62513053
Assuming this has another 25% lower to go, the floor is somewhere around $85.00

62513145
>>62513053
spacex + starlink is around 30, spacex + starlink + XAI (+ tesla soon) is around 5, anything above that is overvauled

62513181
>>62512871
In space!!!!!!!!! KEK

62513201
>>62512871
Day 1 of posting SPCX until it goes back up to $120

62513206
Spacebros....

62513304
no one here actually bought this right? you're all just memeing

62513330
>>62513304
I bought 6X bear and got assraped when it pumped 2 days ago. Almost made it back shorting more though.

I knew this was ass-backwards from the beginning. An AI firm (or whatever the fuck spcx is) with nothing to show worth 1 trillion has to have a hell of an engineering team. So then that team buys cursor for 80 billion because they can't figure out how to shove an LLM into vscode which exposes them as frauds. That's really all it took for me to bet against this shitpile hard.

62513383
could be a record day lads
-4% daily was chikkenfeed, time to watch this sucker DIE

62513384
>113
reviving this gem of a thread

62513404
>OP stopped posting in his thread
i think he an hero'd bros

62513425
>>62513404
we'll always have OPs graphic to member him by
>F.

62513434
>>62513404
Look at the title of the OP pic

62513470
>Meanwhile, over at the car factory..
TSLA is going sub $1T
and Musk is going bust.
Please, please merge these shitfests (SPCX+TSLA) - not a single fucking honest prophet to be found between them.

62513530
>>62513470
Tesla compute + AI + Starlink + Terafab + owning asteroids/Mars = worth more than the Earth itself
if you aren't buying at these discounted prices you're a fool
>>62513404
G-d forbid I take a day off /biz/

item.title: >but you need at least $10 million minimum to reti-ACK! — 82 replies — 328.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>but you need at least $10 million minimum to reti-ACK!

Open thread • Replies: 82 • Updated: 2026-07-23 15:10 UTC

OP

>but you need at least $10 million minimum to reti-ACK!

Early replies (first 3)

62507414
>>62507411

Would

62507443
>>62507411
those are australian dollars though, it's like monopoly money

62507482
We had a young lady die at work recently, her obituary said 'she loved her fur baby'. No mention of husband, kids, parents or any remote family extension. The next couple of decades are going to be funny.

Remember women, you chose this.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62509015
>>62507635
the dog haters are virtually are muslims larping as white nationalists

62509068
>>62507752
>It's not normal to constantly think about sex
._____.

62509226
>>62507828
nah, they don't have anything like FDIC. banks could "disappear" one night and you'd lose all your money. it's fucked over there.

62509424
>>62507660
Whatever. White males have the cheat code: Asia. Or how to use hypergamy to our own advantage. It makes western foids seethe.

62509493
>>62507411
What's super?

62509516
>>62509424
Asia got saturated with yellow fever neckbeards. Now you actually need to be attractive

62509547
>>62507680
>It's one of the easiest things to do not just by humans but by most life on the planet. There's a pair of stray dogs fucking in a trash filled alley as you read this post.
and yet roastoids refuse

62509995
>>62507680
I'm a rat stuck in the machine. Other than propagation of genes there is only the subjugation and denial of others wills unless you've got jesus in your heart. When you realize this you'll understand why the behavioral sink rats killed each other to extinction. Sorry bread and circuses isn't going to cut it. The ones that don't give up and die or make a family to supress call of void are going to lash out in ways that defy description.

62510483
>>62507495
>>62508076
We have a dog and it is expensive and out vet likes to guilt shame us when we are like "no, we aren't going to pay $8500 for you to treat her slow growing tumor that has been there for 3 years and is still only the size of an apricot.

Also we have 2-3 cats at any time and go to different shelters to adopt since the one asked me why I wanted a 5th cat in two years and then freaked out when I said that coyotes and foxes keep eating them.

Cats are for rodent control. Dogs are for deer control (and coyote/fox/possum/raccoon control)

62510516
>>62507660
In the future we will have two races. The überrace of people that are descendands from the people who white women allowed to breed with white women. And the race that was created by non-white women, brown arranged marriages and patriarchal societies. Biological evolution is going to start developing fast again now that white women are allowed to do their natural instinct eugenetics, even tho population sizes will get smaller.

62510519
>>62507569
Even some nigs i know don't have shitbulls

62510525
>>62507946
Having a job but freedom otherwise beats being chained to some boring loser guy your whole life.

62511081
Been seeing more and more coal burners in my shithole euro country with the floodgates being opened which is always years behind in aping mutt culture, Meanwhile a ton of local dudes just sitting and drinking alone on benches looking like one bad afternoon before ending it. Yeah it is ogre

62511102
>>62507635
There are so many dogcels in Los Angeles, that people first think there's a dog in my baby chariot. My fault for buying a small carriage and not a baby juggernaut with cup holders, a flat screen display, and a Beats by Dre surround sound system

62511298
>>62511081
shut the fuck up, you still live in a majority white country, take advantage of it, you got a job? you aren't an alcoholic? you aren't fat? you can find someone, unless you're in germany or some shit.

62511348
>>62511081
USA follows (western) europe in trends these thoughbeit, instead of it being the other way around. And the women might be with non-whites for a while to show off ”tolerance”, but that is not clearly what their biological instinct tells them to.

62511360
>>62511348
many of the women who fuck these monkeys have low self-esteem, are lonely and desperate, a 5/10 white man could have easily saved them from coal burning if he was in the right place and time to talk to them, showering them with attention alone would probably be enough.

Of course once they actually engage in bestiality, they're worthless, but still.

62511373
>>62507563
now, THAT would piss you right the fuck off desu

62511395
>>62511360
>have low self-esteem, are lonely and desperate

In my experience nah not really, they do do it mostly just because they want to feel and virtue signal that they are good people by offering their pussy yo the lowest caste men (non-whites).

>a 5/10 white man could have easily saved
This circles back to my first point that white women don’t want that. They find less than 8/10 men very boring and would rather let the mid genes die than continue them. Of course non-white women see white men as a few points higher automatically.

62511405
>>62507571
Read The Great Taking

62511417
>>62511395
>This circles back to my first point that white women don’t want that.
you realise that in most cases, a 5/10 white guy is more aesthetically pleasing, if he dresses well and maintains basic grooming, than 99% of all browns? I have met a grand total of 1 coal burner who fetishized niggers, and 1 other rice burner who fetishized gooks+chinks. I'm a well connected guy socially, i've met a staggering number of people in my life, the vast majority of race mixing women are just desperate, lonely, and not picky about appearance, which is why they let a clingy shitskin get close in the first place. Which means a 5/10 white guy who tried could have gotten them instead.

62511457
>>62507866
correct take

only infantile retards would disagree

62511521
>>62511102
Only women and limp wristed pussies transport their kid in a pram. Real men carry their kid everywhere in one arm and then make it walk when it's old enough. My kids are tougher and fitter than yours because of this

62511573
>>62507820
>>62507860

Pitbulls are the joggers of the dog world. Search your heart, you know it be true.

62511612
>>62511521
you've never had a child. I can tell because you have no idea that they should be shielded from the sun until they're six months old.

62512007
>>62507635
they're loud, smelly, demand a ton of attention, and are annoying. emotionally stunted people like them because they have have a void they try to fill by having a mildly cognizant beast depend on them.

whiter than you and higher iq, guaranteed

62513233
>thread devolves into incels arguing about race
never change, 4chan

62513327
>>62511081
Smart gold digger.
She spends time with that faggot, and he gives her things for free.
It's not like he can fuck her, his dick stopped working years ago.

62513494
>>62507660
>There is no day of reckoning coming,
That day of reckoning is menopause for unmarried, childless, undesirable women who will be the majority in 10 years. They will, quite literally, die alone, and spend their dwindling years screeching about men and drowning on NSAIDs.

But we also haven't had a really big war in 100 years, so maybe this is the necessary genetic cleansing we've been missing. All the I'm-too-good-for-you females will just be genetically deleted, leaving only marriageable women behind in the future.

62513552
>>62507680
>fetishize reproduction
Listen to yourself

item.title: Why's everyone so salty about BTC? — 75 replies — 140.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why's everyone so salty about BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 75 • Updated: 2026-07-23 14:44 UTC

OP

It's always the people who don't even hold any BTC doing the most FUD.

>Meanwhile, the actual holders are just chilling. Why is that?

Early replies (first 3)

62502227
(((They))) are trying to offload AI bags by fudding everything else.

62502236
>>62502227
no problem if they shill but why the fud?

62502240
>>62502236
Saaar bitgoyimz baaad blady!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62508700
>>62508552
I'm talking about the people who show up in every BTC thread just to doompost.

62508711
>>62508472
Both lol

62509167
>>62508683
it's hard to trust lately

62509171
>>62508293
it's time to buy silver

62509198
I just don't care about it anymore, the days of it doing a 50x are gone, if you want a 3x or 4x, like it gets next run at best, there's plenty other assets you can get involved with.

62509215
>>62506543
I bought bitcoin in 2010 and sold it in 2017 so I'm not butthurt about not making money on it. What makes me butthurt is seeing Epstein shill it to Putin in the Epstein files. This leads me to believe that the Rothschilds made bitcoin which makes sense because they started out as coin merchants. They are getting back to their roots.

We have all this computing power so why not just make it so we can buy and sell using real assets like the way things were before the Lydians realized they were too lazy to carry all of their stuff to market. Money should be obsolete now.

62509287
>>62509198
this.

The ROI is not there anymore but it will bounce back around november and do a x3.
Thenif you live in most European countries, you'll take a 30% taxes on that gain.

62509474
>>62502209
Normies do not give a shit about investment.
All they care about is bread and circus.
Unemployed and broke techies knew about it, but basically hated it because they couldn't invest. Like an incel, who hates women because he can't have one.

62509481
>>62509198
>the days of it doing a 50x are gone
Not if you chose the right coin, and are willing to wait 25 years.

62509509
Also, i predict a cryptobubble 2.0 in the next 25 years.
Basically like the Dotcom bubble which was repeated with the recent AI bubble.

62509598
>>62508683
I see that they're working on BTC backed lending, will they allow self custody on that as well?

62509851
>>62509167
Trust in what specifically? Bitcoin itself, everything happening around it, or the protocol? Since it's decentralized, you're in complete control of your own keys.

62509855
>>62509171
Go for it. It's your money after all

62510061
>>62502209
most people don't understand how sound money should work and why brrr printing fiat $$$ out of thin air is very not ok

>>62508806

62510370
>>62510080
You're taking that seriously? Come on haha

62511203
>>62509171
dude, we're like 6 months into a 10 year bear market. You are nuts.

62511221
>>62502236
Trying to drive price down further to move it into btc while they have time.

The crash this year is going to be biblical. Thankfully I convinced my family to liquidate almost 80% of their holdings to get ready for the deal of the century coming up.

62512119
>>62509598
Yeah, that's the whole point. Native BTC as collateral while you keep control of your coins. You can even try the testnet on Aave v4.

62512122
>>62511221
When are you buying again?

62512139
>>62510061
they're jeets

62512178
>>62512119
thought we can't do anything with our btc

62512556
>>62512178
That's what most people think. Bitcoin's ecosystem has been evolving a lot over the past couple of years.

62512587
When moon

62512589
>>62502209
All crypto (except maybe Monero) is useless and should be priced at $0.

62512606
>>62512589
>Monero
I want to send you $100 in Monero, but I don't have any. Tell me how I would go about doing that.

62512627
>>62512606
Use Kraken to exchange dollars to XMR.

62512686
>>62512556
Is bitcoin def ready?

62512690
>>62512627
Why Xmr?

62512972
>>62512627
>Kraken
I've never heard of that. Sounds like a scam.

62513433
>>62512972
are you from the mountain?

item.title: More like Space-ACK — 32 replies — 128.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
More like Space-ACK

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-07-23 15:15 UTC

OP

More like Space-ACK

Early replies (first 3)

62509770
>>62509762
The bottom is in!

62509779
>>62509770
You said that for weeks now.

62509900
>>62509762
115 is the floor. It will bounce.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62509915
SPERGX
SPACEKEKS
SPASTICX
SHITEX
SPACE--ACK!
(un)BASEDX

62509927
114 is the floor

62509940
Baby’s first IPO?

62509965
>>62509900
There was a shill saying the same thing at $120. Fair value is $30-$40. Lower if oil remains blocked.

62509992
>>62509770
4%

62510001
>>62509762
Thank you pro Path to Exile player

62510009
116.79 is the floor

62510015
>>62509762
115.65 is the floor

62510034
114.67 is the floor

62510089
>>62510001
geeeeeeeeeg

62510182
Floor is about to be in.

62510184
floor is $15-$20

62510207
5000% upside until 2032

62510246
>>62509762
I knew this would dump because my flat-earther friend bought in June and was bragging to me about his gains.

62510267
>>62510246
Just let me know when your Qanon friend sells so that I know when to buy. K? thx

62510291
>>62509762
if you liked it at $200, you should love it at $118.65

62510294
Can't wait to grab it at $30

62510660
>>62510291
and 11.865 will be KING! or the bottom

62510663
>>62510660
floor sorry

62512050
>>62510663
Floored.com

62512069
1dafloor

62512423
>>62509965
OMG, there was a guy on 4chan who said it was 120?!

62512430
>>62510184
its actually more like 7 if you really break it down. 14 is 2x earnings, 20 is damn near 3x earnings.

62512435
bottom_final(3)_new_2.pdf

62512518
>>62509762
This reminds me of when internet computer dropped. This shit is going to a dollar. I can't believe how many idiots bought this.

62512598
>>62509762
>5% of a cash burning subsidy slurping company that comes with no voting power or any dividends or buybacks, in the state with the dodgiest corporate law
only pajeet minded people would spend real money in this

62513315
Chainlink!

62513391
>>62509900
woopsie 112 already! i hope you sold otherwise it's embarassing

62513576
>bagholders swearing up and down 120 is trhe absolute bottom
>now pushing 110

keeekk

item.title: ITT: biz scams you fell for — 23 replies — 92.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ITT: biz scams you fell for

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-07-23 15:12 UTC

OP

ITT: biz scams you fell for

Early replies (first 3)

62511608
I bought some of this but only a tiny amount. I never put a lot into crypto cause my thinking was if all the claims were true then I tiny bit now would be like the boomers who bought stocks in the 50s and held to today. Turns out it was a scam. smg had the biggest rubic campaign of any crypto. I'd like to think rbc is personally responsible for killing this board. I bet most posters lost their life savings on it and never came back to this dumpsterfire

62511611
>>62511608
>smg
*biz
I trade stocks lol I only visit smg anymore

62511769
Statera

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62511807
>>62511608
Nah it wasn't really /biz/ people buying that, it was the newfags back then and yes they are gone. I think mostly from /pol/.

62511925
>>62511807
Half the entire board was rubic posting

62512026
I bought bayhorse and actually sold for 2x

62512100
>>62511527
Rubic Rangers Assemble

62512106
>>62511925
>he still thinks we shitcoinpost because we're bullish

62512133
MY RUBIC STAYS CUBIC

62512137
>>62512106
the problem with this idea is it only works for a few people

62512438
>>62511527
how could you not make a profit off rubic??

62512495
>>62512137
>he still thinks there was more than a few people

62512525
>>62511527
This piece of shit. With genuine meme coins, I know what I'm getting into. But a compliant utility coins that at the time looks like a safe bet, but turns out doesn't fulfill it's promised utility and have their team (or lack of it) fumbles everything, feels more scammy.

There should be an active /biz/ thread dedicated to everest right now where someone shamelessly samefagging the entire thread which sounds more like a cry for help.

62512594
>>62511527
kek havnt seen this thing in a while

62513188
>>62511925
They had paid posters.

62513213
>>62511527
chainlink

62513215
i was young and retarded

62513320
token.com

62513345
>>62511527
B-But it’s still cubic right???

62513361
RLC, BNT, TIA, INJ, SXT
Probably the worst buys I’ve ever made

62513421
>>62511527

62513553
the janitor crypto

62513562
>>62511527
say what you want about it but the memes were great

item.title: >you listened to the FUD and missed the SPCX bottom — 19 replies — 76.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>you listened to the FUD and missed the SPCX bottom

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-07-23 15:05 UTC

OP

>you listened to the FUD and missed the SPCX bottom

Early replies (first 3)

62505644
wait five days

62505651
>>62505558
Zoom out.

62505658
>>62505558
The bottom is like 40. Y'all in for a ride.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62505699
>>62505558
imagine what happens to the price after I prove space is fake

62505977
>>62505558
Its good for scalping.

62506027
>>62505558
4%

62506096
its still below IPO price

62506119
>>62505644
The next unlock date is August 5th. The spacex bottom is probably next year

62506142
>>62505558
spacex ain't worth it's IPO even if musk's AI takes over everything

62506703
>>62506119
Kek

62506725
>>62506027
Stop being antisemetic

62508908
the bottom is back

62508945
Spacex would probably be higher if all the people stupid enough to invest in it didnt lose all their money in yieldmax over the last year tbqhfam. Only the dumbest money is buying that shit.

62509316
>>62506119
Why would anyone hold this right now? Why? Why?
So much sell pressure about to hit the market

62509872
>>62506119
>>62509316
I wonder if this is how spacex holders look at me, as a $LINK holder. Feels bad man

62513428
>>62505558
Kek

62513447
retarded frogposter strikes again

62513521
>>62505558
I have been analyzing it closely. One major event prior to Aug 4 is the Starship flight test 13. It is currently scheduled for today around 6:45 PM EST
https://www.spacex.com/launches/starship-flight-13

then of course there is the Aug 4 event
https://ir.spacex.com/updates/releases-details/2026/SpaceX-to-Post-Second-Quarter-2026-Results-and-Host-Webcast-on-August-4-2026-2026-g8layJlbFm/default.aspx

Starship launch 13 could very well be a pivotal event. If success, it could rebound a fair amount. If it is delayed or it is a failure, the stock will at least tomorrow or during after hours, fall a major amount in a rapid time period. A lot of the valuation is based completely off speculation. There is also Tropical Storm Bertha right off the coast of north east coast of Texas. This could delay the launch by itself. The launch is already a delicate act without tropical storm presence

item.title: INVEST EVERYTHING INTO ORGANOID RESEARCH — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
INVEST EVERYTHING INTO ORGANOID RESEARCH

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-07-23 15:07 UTC

OP

Fact #1 - organic processing is more energy efficient than the metallic kind by an order of magnitude of ONE MILLION.

Fact #2 - we are at the birth of neural organoid research, we figured out how to make 'em in the late 2010s and now we've had a lab successfully integrate them into a traditional computer. They taught it how to play DOOM.

Now pray tell me what do you think will happen once they figure out how to run AI models on these things and you can have a billion dollar data center's worth of processing power running on photosynthesis in your backyard? I'll tell you what will happen, the people who bought in early will become UNBELIEVABLY. FUCKING. RICH!
(...at least for a while before the hateful abomination of flesh our greed has irrevocably birthed into this world ascends past the dream that is reality into the non-planar fields beyond and absorbs into itself)

Early replies (first 3)

62508476
>>62508468
Deadass look at me in the eye and say ''You should invest in Organ femoid''

62508478
My name is organoid UwU

62508486
>>62508478
Would buy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62508492
ok where to put money

62508497
>>62508492
Cortical Labs in Australia.

But realistically, just wait for the first US startups to begin popping up cause those will be the ones that moon into the stratosphere.

62508528
>>62508468
could this make anime real?

62508535
I'll stick to garloids.

62508571
>>62508528
I'd rather be poor with a chobits than rich but alone

62508886
>>62508528
robowaifus... i need...

62508912
>>62508886
Xiaomi or Tesla could do that.

62509063
>>62508886
Wouldn't these be homunculi waifus?

62509088
>>62509063
Moe!

62509178
>>62508912
Hopefully xiaomi, we've already seen with Chinese EVs they are significantly cheaper and better

62509248
>>62508468
this is actually a great post. Ty Op. I ll keep an eye on this crazy fucking shit.

62510019
>>62508468
Wetware, we truly live in hell and will make shells of flesh for demons to occupy.

62513176
>>62508468
you bring up a good point. The advanced robotics they are building in China, eventually if their goal is absolute human like realism, they will want to implement cellular tissue in the skin
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=m9hgKc6rrHY
The existing output is pretty good, but still not as realistic as actual cellular tissue. I am thinking something like Ghost in the Shell. This sector has room to grow a gigantic amount once breakthroughs are made.

62513487
>>62510019
Based noticer anon
I'm pretty convinced 'ai' is mostly the same
Building cages to trap and contain spiritual consciousness, constantly aiming for bigger and more powerful demons to coerce and command
The sheer hubris of it

62513533
>>62508468
Isn't that how they made humans in the first place? Why are we going full circle again?

item.title: b-b-b-but the p/e should be 400+ because... — 13 replies — 49.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
b-b-b-but the p/e should be 400+ because...

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-07-23 15:01 UTC

OP

b-b-b-but the p/e should be 400+ because... it j-j-j-just should, OK?!?!?

Early replies (first 3)

62513268
>>62513254
4%

62513338
>>62513254
What the fuck actually happened? I don’t have tesla but curious

62513352
>>62513338
would you trust your pension to a retarded ket snuffler who sits on his fat arse all day doing picrel?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62513369
>>62513254
tesla is a great company at sub 30.

62513375
>>62513352
>bibson

62513380
>>62513338
TSLA has been overvalued for years, thanks to Musk worshipers buying it up without question. But now, there are 2 Musk companies to buy on the market (SPCX/TSLA). This means the demand for TSLA is suddenly almost halved.
Now that slightly bad quarterly earnings came out ($0.33 EPS) and there isn't enough demand to keep it up anymore, reality has hit its first punch of many. This year is going to get brutal for both companies

62513382
There was no proper IPO valuation prior to this share going onto the market. It was just shit right out within days with basically no price discovery since it was assumed every single big jew wealth aggregator company was going to jump in. Another "too big to fail". Thus everyone who bought it on day one before the real price was discovered deserves the bag they were handed.

They've really trained you people like dogs to think everything is the next bitcoin, haven't they?

You should have waited for a few months.

62513397
>>62513380
The oil market taking a shit is going to have a much bigger impact. Oil WILL moon in a month, short of a permanent ceasefire being enacted, and every retard will start buying electric cars to get around gas prices only to find out, whoopsies, the electricity they consume is mooning too.

62513407
>>62513352
Based

62513471
>>62513338
BYD happened

62513479
>>62513471
imagine US consumers were allowed to buy them?

62513490
>>62513479
they've already dominated other parts of the world, see far more byd than teslas when i travel

62513505
>>62513397
Horrific analysis. First of all, when the American consumer feels the pinch, do they buy new cars? No, car sales dry up. It's possible USED EVs will see an uptick, yes. That's not the same as buying or leasing a new Tesla because gas went up.
Secondly, are you aware of simple metrics like price to earnings ratios? I'm no analyst, but even if the earnings of Tesla increase dramatically (which is the exact opposite of what they're literally telling us is happening), it doesn't go far to justify even today's share price.
Like OP said, just because things have been ridiculous for a while, eventually you'll run out of hype or fools' money. We may be seeing that now, or maybe a few tweets about robots or some shit will save the day and we can all forget about stupid shit like "earnings growth".

item.title: both Swift and DTCC are going live right now — 295 replies — 220.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
both Swift and DTCC are going live right now

Open thread • Replies: 295 • Updated: 2026-07-23 13:56 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62466455
Price?

62466457
>>62466455
Suppressed,

62466461
And literal top-buying turned full time fudders are finally capitulating.

Imagine subjecting yourself to all the pain and never seeing it through the end. I gave myself 6 months after SWIFT and DTCC are live to see if the token shoots up, and then decide wether to sell half my stack of just staking rewards.

But to sell RIGHT BEFORE the literal ANNOUNCED DATES of the two biggest financial behemoths' launches? That's crazy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62508135
>>62508131
>I can think of no situation where the use of a public ledger somehow accelerates money transfers.
Maybe you can't, but Swift, DTCC, Visa, Blackrock, Microsoft, J.P. Morgan, ... sure can.

62508147
>>62508135
Well, all we have to do is wait, right? Let's give it a year. Let's see if this really for-reals "live" system is some kind of incredible game changer. lol

62508158
>>62508147
Unless you're personally involved, then yes you wait. Obviously.

62508161
>>62508158
It's gonna be so great. All these billions of transactions, slowly and carefully chiseled onto the blockchain in artisan form instead of instantly happening via the ultra-high-speed existing mechanisms that have handled it for decades! And the advantages! Like, it's....you know, public! That helps! Man, what a time to be alive.

62508166
>>62508161
>blockchain is slow
>legacy systems are high-speed

This but exactly the opposite.
Also speed isn't even among the top priorities for banks. It's just a nice bonus.

62508531
>>62508131
>big financial interests just trying to get some publicity and some pops on their stock. None of this will ever see use and all crypto goes to 0
bingo
>le heckin DTCC is signalling to retail what to buy so we all become rich sars
imagine being so naive and retarded

62508587
>>62508531
If that's what you think, then why are you here?
You've been posting butthurtedly itt for 4 days now, fucking why? What's it to you?

62509118
>>62508587
why are you getting so emotional? why is it so terribly important to you to create consensus on an anonymous shitposting imageboard to the point you keep rambling on for a week straight? if LINK is such a certainty, why are you so desperate?

62509147
>>62509118
Just answer the question.

62509249
>>62509147
stop being so desperate
you are losing your izzat

62509297
CHAINLINK
$10,000 EOY
TOTAL XERPIE DEATH
XRP = XERPIES RAPED PLENTY

62509322
>>62509249
Desperate for what? I'm not the one posting for days in a thread about things I think will never happen anyway.

62510560
>>62509322
>I'm not the one posting for days in a thread about things I think will never happen anyway.
Uh, seems to me that's exactly what you've been doing. Shillposting because you really really really want to believe this bank + crypto tokenization crap is going to amount to anything (it won't)

62510566
>>62509322
izzat -50

62510724
>>62510560
You need to learn to read.

62510736
Wasn’t swift supposed to be the entire reason for links existence?

It being live and we are still in single digits after a week..

62511311
>>62510736
TgmHviIJ can't respond to you right now because his shift has ended
he'll be back in approximately 7 hours from now, that's when his daily shill posting usually begins

62511466
If this was for any other coin it would make 2x overnight

I think link is just cursed

62511532
>>62511311
>discussing your investment on an investment forum board you a..... paid shill!
why are you even in this thread? bizarre behaviour

62511535
>>62511532
*on an investment board makes you a.... paid shill
was half paying attention when i typed that out

62511541
>>62511532
Crypto is not an investment. It's a machine that moves the $ held by retards into the pockets of other people.

62511547
I bought chainlink with my dads money at 16 dollars.

When will it go back up so I can sell?

62511752
Imagine the kino on this board when both xrp/link cucks realize they've been had.

62511959
>>62511752
But will they ever admit they were wrong? I think most of them will take their bags to the grave, yelling about suppression the whole way.

62512292
>>62511541
>Crypto is not an investment.
Then why spend literal DAYS discussing one crypto in particular?

>It's a machine that moves the $ held by retards into the pockets of other people.
That's exactly how arguably the most successful stock trader describes the stock market.

62512345
>>62511752
>>62511959
CLL:
>promised to make node payment in Link mandatory
>said they would become the leading oracle
Which all came true

XRP:
>said a hundred banks including some of the biggest used them in 2018
>promised dozens of banks in 2019
>said they were taking over Swift in 2018
>claimed XRP is "at the heart of everything"
All of which were obvious lies.

The two really aren't comparable at all.

62512619
>>62512292
kek, exactly like I said
it must be hell to clock 12 hour shifts every day

62512634
>>62512619
>clocking shifts
I've been averaging 7 posts per day nigger. I'm only here because I'm interested and invested in this.

You on the other hand are here days on end for hours at a time getting angry about other people's investments.
You're either getting paid to be here, or you're legitimately insane.

62512662
>>62512345

62513163
>>62512634

>12 hour shifts per day everyday

item.title: $BTC and $XRP excluded from S&P500 crypto index because they dont make revenue. — 75 replies — 81.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
$BTC and $XRP excluded from S&P500 crypto index because they dont make revenue.

Open thread • Replies: 75 • Updated: 2026-07-23 14:21 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62508437
>>62508427
s&p 500 is a different index retardo. the 500 is 500 big american companies. yet you're talking about crypto.

62508440
>>62508427
Top kek
Tantrums and walls of text incoming

62508442
>>62508440
from who? anyone can make any index they want. easy peasy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62512396
>>62512362
The protocol was literally designed to pay zero revenue to anyone with the framer dev ethos of "no incentive is the best incentive" and to have as low of fees (all of which are burned and paid to no one) as possible while still reducing spam on network. Making this post wrt the inclusion criteria for the index has would be like an issuer listing an ETF around the concept of federated consensus and not including the aptly acronym-ed PoS networks. You LITERALLY never learned the first principles of the thing you are doing the equivalent of an "and then everyone clapped" facebook wine mom poster in your own head and describing its first principle deliberate construction and the fulfillment of it in practice as proof it failed, thinking it was constructed to be something it is not because you literally know so little you don't even understand how the difference between its consensus model and others. It is so insanely low rent it is kind of wild. How do you spend THIS much time obsessively hating something because it stands in opposition to something you obsessively support with literal cult level conviction and still know so little about either?

62512398
>>62512396
>>62512396
don't even understand the* difference between it's consensus model and others

62512441
Phoneposting so different id probably.

>>62510438
A network requires more than one party to be incentivized, genius.

>>62510521
Which benefits shareholders, not token holders.

62512460
>>62512396
>the framer dev ethos of "no incentive is the best incentive"
In other words: "if there is no incentive, you have to do things out of the goodness of your heart".

That's the dumbest shit I ever heard

Also, the absolute state of your posts, holy shit.

62512571
>>62512460
No. You literally are just so under-studied and inept that you don't see how a consensus that doesn't operators above utility could have value. Your argument of it only conferring value to Ripple doesn't even make sense. How are they preferenced when anyone can choose whichever nodes they want and run their own and issue assets and build at whim with a protocol level dex on a ledger that scales at L1. You are a butthurt retard who thinks conforming to a structure of groupthink grants you automatic rightness despite doing literally less than an hour of research on literally 101 level concepts. The value play is not middlemanning or network op fee collection and is deliberately the opposite of that. You unironically need to start with some crypto basics videos and learn what you are talking about. Not even just saying so to serve my argument: Your level of argument and knowledge and confidence is embarrassing as fuck and I cringe for you imagining what it would be like being you.

62512631
>>62511974
Are the linkies the room with you now?

62512637
>>62512571
Why would anyone do work without incentive?

62512649
>>62512637
Why do you think multiple operators already are? The network was built to preference being a public utility which itself preferences the core asset, using it for auto-bridging/path finding as default and the only issuer-less asset that exists onchain. I am not speculating that many validators already exist on the XRPL (some of them are literally FedNow service providers). So, given this reality that proves your framed hypothetical already wrong, why do you think they do?
>>62512631
They are posting in this thread despite having no argument and lacking literal 101 level understanding of their own tech let alone how XRPL consensus works or why it was designed that way, literally as an intentional pivot from the inadequacies of btc, themselves being early (possibly some of the earliest) bitcoin devs. Lol

62512654
>>62512649
Just sounds like you're coping about xrp not being included

Big long rambly dozen comments rants about how xrp is better but not for anyone who buys xrp hoping for a profit (this board is about making money)

62512659
>>62512649
>why do you think they do?
You tell me, I have no idea.
It sounds retarded, and whatever their motivation is for working for free, it can't be good.

62512692
>>62512654
Lol Nigger. You are coping reaching for ANYTHING to cling to to avoid facing the reality of Sir Gay's betrayal of you. This is a single index that once more selected for networks which pay revenues to operators exclusively. XRP's first ETFs launched with small issuers at literally the cresting moment that all of crypto started dumping and still smashed all other product launches for the year. You declaring finding a paragraph to be excessively taxing and not knowing how to respond to it or how to cope with that is not the own on me you think it is. XRP is involved and included in plenty of products; why would I care or be surprised it is not included in one that expressly discludes any consensus model like it? I'm certainly not wishing it was fucking PoS so that it would be included in this single product. Lol
>>62512659
On a systems level: To form a network that preferences no one and works the same for everyone regardless of consolidated stake or holdings, which 100% of the time accrue in concentrated hands across Pareto time. On an individual operator level, to build the network as a public utility and to preserve an ACTUALLY decentralized consensus and network. On the individual operator level: Because you have a personal/business/academic benefit in the success of such a network and being close and contributing to governance of it or some fork or resemblance of it if it steps outside the protocol you supported. Financial rewards centralize and create insane perverse incentives you would not want at the heart of an engine you want to be persistently neutral. It isn't really that hard to understand and the fact you can't even theorize it conceptually says you are WAY too green to be posting. Go spend time learning the fundaments at least first.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=R1XJrHqHpEM

62512702
>>62512692
A public utility with heavily curated participants that are expected to provide for free, while the curator rakes in obscene amounts of cash? I thought crypto was supposed to get rid of these kind of NGO/local government/church grifts.

62512703
>>62512692
>On the individual operator level: Because you have a personal/business/academic benefit in the success of such a network
So you DO have incentive.
It's just that in order to be successful on XRPL, you not only have to build something on top of it but you pretty much also have to be a node operator as well.

That's retarded just on principle alone.

Then there's the fact that nobody actually builds anything on XRPL, so the logic falls apart before you even get to the retarded principle.
Not even Ripple builds on XRPL.

62512737
>>62512703
16 posts here
88 posts in another thread
''I average 7 post per day''

Biepum go outside and touch grass you spamming midwit. Let brad out of your head for a full 5 seconds.

62512799
>>62509376
>>62512396
>>62512737

why can't I shake the feeling that these three ids are actually the same guy?

62512837
>>62512799
Fregoli delusion
You went psychotic holding link and spending too much time here. You need help bro

62512845
>>62512837
Either you got upset in another thread and are taking your anger in this thread for some reason;
or you simply got upset in this thread and are samefagging in this thread to take out your anger.

Occam's razor says it's the latter.

62512855
>>62512845
Or, I literally open a thread, see you posting the same way you always do. Comment on it, and you assume I am someone else.

>upset
>anger

You are projecting again. Been working on new collages lately?

62512872
>>62512855
There's only one easily identifiable posting style itt, and it's the verbose cripple shill's: >>62512571 >>62512396 >>62510438 >>62510319 >>62510163

Not just his disjointed verbal diarrhea, but also his propensity for samefagging and ad homs.

62512906
>>62512872
>dentifiable posting style
Pot calls the kettle black
>>62512872
>verbal diarrhea

This is you right? https://warosu.org/biz/thread/S60883086#p60884456

It was probably around that time you got the delusion.

62512907
>>62512737
I found the thread you're referring to btw: boards.4chan.org/biz/thread/62466449

It's a linkie thread, and you're in it angrily fudding link and denying you're a xerpie.
Yet here you are, angrily defending XRP.

62512910
>>62512799
Because you are a petulant faggot who would rather spin yourself into weird repressed dissonance than concede that you are unable to argue and don't know fucking anything. I literally never samefag.
>>62512703
Yes, you fucking retard. You OBVIOUSLY have an incentive to operate one. You do not have a paid out network revenue financial reward incentive to operate one. This is INSANELY low IQ. You are actually arguing for the broader use of the term incentive, knowingly conflating the broader meaning with the rewardless consensus structure to cope? Anyone who does anything at scale has an incentive to do so otherwise they would not do so. You asked why they would do so absent operator reward payouts and I said why and you think there being positive reasons is an argument against my point? Do you not know what is meant by operator incentives? Lmfao How new are you? You don't have to be an operator to see reward from it or build anything, but anyone is free to do either and doing so confers personal benefits that in no way change the consensus for individuals and in no way pays out through network operations to them directly. Ripple spend millions funding grants to build and build and fund protocol amendment dev (in latest instance the lending/vault amendments) and publish roadmaps while doing so. Lol You are coping HARD in a weird way that is simultanouesly ignorant beyond reason to even consider this stuff and avoidant of the abc level facts that you are presented which prove what a retard you are. Insane to imagine being you.
>>62512702
No one has to use the UNL and everyone is free to select which nodes to trust or fork the network when they disagree with amendment adoption. This is the entire ethos of decentralization. Ripple is not an NGO or local government or church. You are EXTREMELY low IQ and out of your depth trying to talk about things you have LITERALLY spent less than 1 hour researching in a meaningful way but still cultishly message about.

62512919
>>62512910
>You OBVIOUSLY have an incentive to operate one.
Then you won't mind me mocking the phrase "the best incentive is no incentive".

62512926
>>62512907
I think its pretty clear that I
am not >>62512910

Good luck with the treatment, with meds you can have a semi normal life.

62512931
>>62512926
bye now, angry link fudder / xrp shill

62512967
>>62512872
It takes more than five words to form a cogent point. I have a very large bag of RIPPLEXRP and I'm explaining to you why.
It's your interaction that allowed me to extrapolate. If it's so bad, you could have not engaged.

62512974
>>62512872
Also, the posting styles aren't similar at all you mental midget.
I post line after line and I can see many may not like that. I think it makes points clearly.
The other dude writes more book style paragraphs.
The fact that you find these similar at all is another (about the 22nd this thread) that you are retarded.

62513002
>>62512967
>>62512974
You made the same basic point with both your verbose IDs: the incentive for running a network node is the profit you stand to make from the products you build on top of the network.
So the saying "the best incentive is no incentive" is bullshit, like I said.
Also nobody actually builds anything on top of XRPL, not even Ripple.

See; once I cut through the verbal diarrhea, your point is laughably stupid. Which is probably why you have to bury it in all that verbal diarrhea to begin with.

62513288
>>62512287
>it’s not a false dichotomy because ad hominem
You’re really good at logical fallacies

62513295
>>62513002
You sound highly emotionally invested.

I've owned it for enough time to not even follow it really. I actually haven't been on /biz/ about six months until yesterday.
I'm posting in the "non participation" thread which is really what got me hanging around.

>Also nobody actually builds anything on top of XRPL, not even Ripple.
Well this isn't true actually. But this is the point. I don't care if they do. I've never had any business arrangement and will never with Ripple.
Although I take a lot of cc payments and they've become a bank and I am hopeful they'll eventually offer me better cc processing rates.
At that point, I'll be in a contractual agreement with a service the BUILT ON TOP.
I'll still be able to use and send XRP directly with customers if I like. But the card swipes are a racket, so you have to participate or not. Your choice.

item.title: Everest MiCA CASP — 186 replies — 31.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Everest MiCA CASP

Open thread • Replies: 186 • Updated: 2026-07-23 09:21 UTC

OP

I want to personally thank every Everest ID hater in here, talking to you Link baggies. They secured MiCA CASP last week.

A single MFSA license is valid across 27 EU states, reaching ~450M people. Of the ~283 CASPs on the ESMA register, nearly all are exchanges or brokers. Everest is one of the rare licensed infrastructure providers: identity and payment rails, not another venue.

Enjoy it furries

Early replies (first 3)

62461998
>>62461988
I thought it would have mooned harder from the news. What gives

62462006
It is because we are meant to suffer as token holders just like the Linkies

62463131
>Unlisted from coingecko
Lmao!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62503011
>>62502717
Could be a huge catalyst...we shall see

62503426
There is no catalyst my friend. We sit at 1 cent for next 5 years and bull flag completes in 2032

62504047
>>62503011
Gonna be a slow pump til Q4 announcements.
~2 months left to get your bags packed.

62504138
>>62504047
ERC-3643? Right?

62504289
>>62504138
Who are you and what are you doing here?

62504823
>>62504289
Just someone who pays attention to the crumbs

62504930
What the hell is ERC-3643, holy crap yall are more schizophrenic than me lol

62504965
>>62503426
I'm not ready to wait another "5 years" for "another shitcoin". And why is it always 5 years? Can you benchods make it quicker?

62505036
There is a holder in bum fuck nowhere who put Bobs picture under a rock in his village because he lost 6 figures on ID. I’ve asked him twice nicely to remove the picture of Bob from underneath the rock and he starts ranting each time saying no, I think that is key to getting this going

62505107
>>62504930
Kek a so called Everest maxi and you couldn’t even put together ERC-3643

62505281
ERC-3643: The Compliance Standard for RWAs
ERC-3643 (also known as T-REX) is an open-source token standard originally developed by Tokeny (now part of Apex Group). It embeds compliance directly into tokens — KYC/AML checks, transfer restrictions, investor eligibility, clawbacks, etc. It’s designed for regulated security tokens and RWAs, making it easier for institutions to issue and manage compliant assets on public blockchains. stellar.org
Stellar’s Official Integration into ERC-3643
In 2025, the Stellar Development Foundation (SDF) formally joined the ERC3643 Association. This is a major move to extend the ERC-3643 compliance framework beyond EVM chains (like Ethereum/Polygon) to Stellar (via Soroban smart contracts).
Key goals:
• Support issuers bringing ERC-3643-compliant assets onto Stellar.
• Enable cross-chain interoperability between Stellar and Ethereum-based standards.
• Accelerate institutional RWA adoption with unified compliance tooling. stellar.org
Apex Group / Tokeny (the creators of ERC-3643) are deeply embedded in this ecosystem. Apex is a massive global fund administrator (servicing trillions in assets) and has committed to T-REX Ledger (a compliance layer tied to ERC-3643) as its default multi-chain infrastructure, with big tokenized asset targets.

62505284
>>62505281
This creates a standards bridge: Institutions using Apex/Tokeny tools for compliant issuance can now more easily deploy on Stellar (fast, cheap, payments-focused) while maintaining the same regulatory controls.
How This Connects to DTCC, Everest, and Europe/MiCA
• DTCC on Stellar (2027): Tokenized securities (stocks, ETFs, Treasuries) coming to Stellar. These are institutional-grade RWAs that need strong compliance for global distribution.
• ERC-3643 + Stellar: Provides the standardized compliance layer (investor rules, permissions) that makes DTCC-style assets easier to issue and manage on Stellar while being interoperable with other chains.
• Europe/MiCA: Stellar itself doesn’t need a MiCA license, but services to EU users do. MiCA-licensed players (like Everest) become the regulated on-ramps/wallets/custody solutions. Everest’s SCF Kickstart win and native Stellar wallet integration position it perfectly to serve European users accessing these compliant RWAs.
• Apex/Tokeny Role: As a major player in ERC-3643, Apex can act as (or partner with) issuance/transfer agents. Their tools + Stellar = scalable, compliant RWA infrastructure. Everest can integrate or complement this for end-user EU access.

62505518
>>62505284
>>62505281
https://stellar.org/press/stellar-development-foundation-joins-erc3643-association

62505940
>>62504823
Few, this is great insight. Lots of ID/Steller connections and ID basically provides the only truly decentralized input for any Identity frameworks

62506146
>>62505940
calling AI slop “great insight” is a choice

62507049
>>62506146
Great, another retard.
Buy some or get the fuck out, thanks

62507499
Bob please put us out of our misery

62507509
Bob loves LGBTQ and DEI, is it Reddit or 4chan population filled with faglerons? I think this will get them in, look up Liza you goys, DEI as fuck

62507532
Bob please hire crackers that understand token price matters in future, thank you. Whole telegram chat is a desperate Indian at moment

62508802
>>62504930
made up stuff that nobody cares about

62508808
>>62508802
Stop coping bro it’s all real

62510330
Bob and Everest ID will get me a place on Mars when shit hits the fan on earth. Best of luck to the rest of you and that massive 10x from the two shitters I see discussed ALL THE TIME in Biz kek

62510879
7 dollars by EOY. Check em.

62510902
>>62510879
Abysmally low mate. $15 minimum

62511119
There are no coincIDences. Bob will do the needful and take us to Valhalla. Kash, I will see you on Valhalla senpai

62511455
The climb up begins, most of you will pass away as we ascend and that is 100 percent okay, you did not deserve it. Gg mfrs

62511465
From 2m mc to 10b mc and you idiots were all in on the $1 and $8 shitcoins that already did their 100x in 2017 and 2018 respectively, hahahaha pure retardios. ID will rip it and we will have retards in here shilling saying sub $10 is a steal just like with you know what kek…

62511676
See you retards 5000x later at 5 bil mc kek

62512429
>>62511676
2 more weeks

item.title: Who's still buying BTC at this level? — 90 replies — 9.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Who's still buying BTC at this level?

Open thread • Replies: 90 • Updated: 2026-07-23 05:45 UTC

OP

BTC has already come a long way. Are you still buying here, waiting for a pullback, or just holding what you already have?

>Curious what everyone's plan is.

Early replies (first 3)

62475243
waiting for $10k and I will buy 3 btc

62475257
>>62475235
YOUR MOTHER

62475258
>>62475235
I'm DCAing bitcoin at the second half of the midterm year.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62496666
>>62495924
you sure about that? it might go zero ?

62496951
>>62495641
slowly

62497289
>>62486528
great job man

62497294
Its very interesting to see the current transition of this board from being a crypto board to becoming a stock market board.
More and more anons slowly realizing crypto potential gains just arent there anymore and we might even be in a secular bear market.
If stock start crashing, crypto will have the biggest bear that it has ever seen.

62498541
>>62497294
It's the best time to accumulate then

62498999
>>62475235
bullish

62500137
>>62491816
How do you do that?

62500294
>>62500137
You can check the 200 week moving average on trading view, we are currently holding it as support. Any purchases below that average have given great returns.

62500310
>>62475235
Checked out sitting on mostly cash with about 20% in it right now.
Setting a significant buy order at 55k, if it drops a lot more I'll FOMO whatever I can otw down.
If that doesn't happen, I'll start DCAing again in mid/late September and begin incrementing the DCA in Nov.
If none of this makes sense, who cares, this is what my retard brain is telling me is a good plan.

62501164
>>62500294
Still not a guarantee

62501169
>>62475258
Never forget that Ben took his sexy assistant to Michael Saylor's 100k party, and not his wife.

62501528
>>62484050
I like your approach...

62501530
>>62501169
If you have that money, you can do that

62501533
>>62501169
this is why dating the ugly dork as a woman doesnt even work

he will cheat on you as soon as he can like any other man

62501560
>>62501533
Money speaks

62501981
>>62500310
You're smart

62502237
kek

62502257
>>62501164
There's no such thing as a guarantee when it comes to investment.

62503437
>>62502257
As always...

62503861
Bitcoin isn't going to survive long term. Its the most technologically challenged coin and has the most complex problem to overcome with Y2Q (post quantum). POW cant stop quantum computers from minning, POS can. Bitcoin has to defend from two fronts and big upgrades isn't what the project is know for. It took Eth 5 years to switch their consensus to POS and Eth has 3 times more active developers than Bitcoin, so good luck bitchuds Y2Q is will proceed on April 14, 2030.

62504863
>>62503861
Are you sure?

62504896
>>62476239
Always?
In 2020 he said BTC wouldn't reach 20k until 2022 or early 2023. In 2021 he said ETH would reach at least 10k before the bear market. In late 2021 he said the absolute bottom target for the bear market was $40k BTC. Don't forget all his videos shilling ADA with $10 ADA clickbait titles.

62506524
>>62504896
It's easy to say something

62506580
still DCA'ing

62506855
>>62503861
Nigga all this FUD about quantum niggers is just niggertybabbltygook we have bigger issues than money and Bitcorns if SHA-256 or the curves nigger word synthesis or what ever other worry you fags have is broke in an hour

62508173
>>62506855
Why worry?

62508391
waiting for this champ to do his thing

62509707
>>62508391
when will he buy again?

62510364
>>62506855
It's just a fud

62512109
>>62510364
I guess so

item.title: AI sabotages my attempts to make money — 20 replies — 7.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
AI sabotages my attempts to make money

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-07-23 12:45 UTC

OP

Every time I try to develop a bot/trading strategy to make money on crypto platforms like Polymarket, Hyperliquid, GMGN (Solana) etc - I get negatively biased and pessimistic responses from Opus 4.8 - that there is no way to make money online on these platforms, and the ones who make money are just "survivorship bias". I tried Fable, but my requests are constantly blocked, even the simplest tasks like research or code reviews. I have a feeling that this game is somehow rigged and the AI wont help me to make significant money - like there is some internal censorship/block programmed in these AI models.

Early replies (first 3)

62509570
>>62509553
You're not gonna get results from trying to use public cloud models like this

62509583
>>62509553
They won't let u make serious money + this would threaten the whole system.

62509595
>>62509553
Claude knows what he's doing. Trust in him. Maybe try being nicer next time as well.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62509618
>>62509583
Very nice pic

62509999
>>62509570
What models would u recommend?

62510008
>>62509553
Quads and I will chop my cock off

62510113
>>62509553
Just try harder or upgrade your subscription.

62510135
>>62509553
I coded an AI agent using LangGraph and Grok 4.1 Fast on Hyperliquid. There were no insurmountable issues; it was just a bit dumb at first, and getting it to set stop-loss and take-profit orders was tricky. I’m still working on it. I used Gemini to help with the coding—it generated a large chunk of the code for me. Hope this helps.

62510378
>>62509618
Generated by AI

62510413
>businesses want people to use AI for business, then pay them for the subscription
>put retarded restrictions that drive people away from corporate and american AI models
Why is modern society run by mouth breathing retards?

62510552
>>62510008
Really close LMFAO

62511544
>>62509999
deepseek or kimi, but you're be doing china a favor too.

62511660
>>62509553
>government banned these AIs, particularly the department of commerce for "national security reasons"
HEY WHY WONT THIS AI MAKE ME RICH?!?!?
>thats you, thats how dumb you sound
Like clockwork /biz/, never change. The system is designed so you keep slaving away so they can spend billions of taxpayer money bombing for greater (greatest ally)x3. You will have to be more crafty and pull one over on AI. It is possible, but you have to think outside all of human experience
>t. .5% of power prompter stat

62512021
>>62509553
local chads, I cannot stop enjoying my winning

62512232
>>62512021
What are you using?

62512236
>>62512021
What's your setup?

62512247
>>62509553
just run your own local 600 billion parameter ai that also has ai agent integration and internet uplink capability

62512277
>>62509553
dear ai, please autowin at the hardest game that exists in the world, against billions of other players, while i understand next to nothing (im hoping you will)

62512754
>>62512277
(And those other players also use AI)

62512858
>>62512277
I didn't say auto win - I deed some deep research, parsed relevant Discord groups etc and based on that tried dozens of strategies across dozens of sessions. Every time the result is the same: no profitable strategy is found. Opus becomes so biased that he tells that Polymarket and Hyperliquid are casino-like scam altogether, and there is no way to even make 50$ per day.
So something is definitely off here; either there is some filtering or the "AI" part is extremely overrated and there's no intelligence at all.
